title: der mond
wikitext:
[]: der mond             Quote Post by ice reaper » Tue May 03, 2005 11:08 am i keep rolling 'em out. anyone know if der mond is no longer being printed? i've been looking for it for months and months and nowhere has it including viz! i need help!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue May 03, 2005 11:39 am It looks like you'll have to try to pick it up second-hand. There aren't even any open offers on amazon UK.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 03, 2005 11:50 am Or you can do what I did and get it off Yahoo! Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue May 03, 2005 2:44 pm I see it anime cons on occasion. Its not the stuff of myths, but it is indeed difficult to find. It is in circulation, but its no longer in print. If you want to see another printing, I suggest you PM Carl Horn and ask him to snap his fingers and make Viz do another printing. Aside from that, happy hunting. *cuddles his Viz edition of Der Mond*

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue May 03, 2005 4:00 pm Try looking in Barnes&nobles used books section. They have connections with small bookstores that lets them have a bigger selection with more unique choices. Thats how I got mine at least. Too bad I still can't understand a word of it though. :(

[]:             Quote Post by mike » Tue May 03, 2005 7:48 pm It was reprinted recently in France so you can get it off amazon.fr pretty easily.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue May 03, 2005 8:14 pm or just go to Evamade, Polyx Terijima just put up scans of the whole thing

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed May 04, 2005 1:50 pm If you want to see another printing, I suggest you PM Carl Horn and ask him to snap his fingers and make Viz do another printing.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Wed May 04, 2005 6:59 pm He no longer works there, he's with Dark Horse, but you could E-mail Viz, and be like "Yo! I want my Der Mond.....you know with the Rei on the cover...and pretty pictures inside....."

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Thu May 05, 2005 10:23 am i've pmed him and he said that he's going to try his best to......well i don't know- get it printing again or get me a copy? either could happen.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu May 05, 2005 2:13 pm Now you said about Der Mond i was looking about it too and i didn't find it..I went to animebooks.com and it has a book called "Neon Genesis Evangelion Illustration and Artwork Collection"..Is this something similar to Der Mond?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 05, 2005 2:33 pm I am embarassed to say, even I have no idea what is included in that one.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Thu May 05, 2005 2:34 pm ....prolly has some Eva artwork though...../sarcasm....

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu May 05, 2005 3:01 pm More than likely it's just a lite rehash of images found in all of the other books.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu May 05, 2005 4:03 pm Reichu wrote: I am embarassed to say, even I have no idea what is included in that one.

[]:             Quote Post by mike » Thu May 05, 2005 4:43 pm It mostly has very large versions of the colored drawings on the Manga, I'd recommend getting Die Sterne since it has more original previously unseen content.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 06, 2005 1:47 am mike wrote: It mostly has very large versions of the colored drawings on the Manga, I'd recommend getting Die Sterne since it has more original previously unseen content.

[]:             Quote Post by mike » Fri May 06, 2005 5:41 am Yeah, I was just assuming the regular Western Eva fan doesn't own all of the Sadamoto stuff from Newtype Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by questforsteve » Thu Nov 26, 2009 10:33 pm Try [url]barnesandnoble.com[/url] and check the store availability, if there isn'y a copy available near you, just special order it and have it delivered to the store (shipping is cheaper that way)

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Nov 26, 2009 10:38 pm Considering this is a four year old thread that and the thread starter isn't active... I'm sure he or she won't be looking at this thread for your response.
